{"credits": [{"type": "remediation_developer", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2144837?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/alexandre-daubois", "id": 2144837, "login": "alexandre-daubois", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjIxNDQ4Mzc=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/alexandre-daubois", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-45070", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 4.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-93", "name": "Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')"}], "description": "### Description

`Symfony\Component\Mime\Header\ParameterizedHeader` (and the related parameter handling reachable from `Symfony\Component\Mime\Header\Headers`) is responsible for serializing structured headers such as `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition`, which carry `key=value` parameters (e.g. `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="x"`).

RFC 2045 / RFC 5322 require parameter *names* to be `tokens`: a restricted ASCII subset that excludes whitespace, CR/LF, and the `tspecials` set. Symfony's parameter handling validates and properly encodes parameter *values*, but does not validate parameter *names*: the supplied name is emitted verbatim into the serialized header.

A caller that derives a parameter name from untrusted input, e.g. an application that lets a user influence a `Content-Disposition` parameter name, can include `\r\n` or other non-token bytes inside the name, terminating the current header and injecting additional headers in the rendered message. This is the classic CRLF / header-injection primitive applied to the parameter-name slot.

### Resolution

`ParameterizedHeader` now rejects parameter names that contain bytes outside the RFC `token` character class.

The patch for this issue is available [here](https://github.com/symfony/symfony/commit/e62ea217f8b4ca8ae922ad0f949e0c4dc1f9b613) for branch 5.4.

### Credits

Symfony would like to thank Fabian Fleischer for reporting the issue and Alexandre Daubois for fixing it.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00292, "percentile": 0.21174}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-vqc8-7275-q272", title: Symfony has Email Header Injection via Non-Token Characters in Mime Parameter Names
